commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=aac90d06e0251cbb5a17e294d721c07e0b7dca22)
commands.review_summary:
The PR ports runtime-aware provider selection, static-catalog fallback, discovery retry, and auth routing into `/btw`, with expanded unit tests.

PR surface: Source +80, Tests +68. Total +148 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main’s registry-only `/btw` path and the submitted OpenClaw 2026.6.5 output both reproduce the unknown-model failure under the stated configuration.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is a concrete resolver defect: the branch still fails the original configuration and needs focused code, regression-test, and live-proof repair.

target.title: fix(agents): align /btw model resolution with runtime aliases
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92168.

`/btw` used a registry-only model lookup, while the main agent loop resolves models through `resolveModelAsync` with runtime-aware static catalog fallback. That made `/btw` fail for canonical provider aliases such as `anthropic/*` when they are intentionally routed through a CLI runtime via `agentRuntime.id` and no direct provider entry exists in the agent's discovered `models.json`.

This PR aligns `/btw` model resolution with the main agent path by:

- resolving the selected harness/runtime before model lookup
- using `resolveModelAsync` instead of the primitive registry-only resolver
- allowing bundled static catalog fallback when a plugin/CLI runtime owns transport
- preserving the existing discovered-models retry path for normal OpenClaw-owned transports

## Real behavior proof

Reproduced the user-visible fix on a real OpenClaw 2026.6.5 install (Telegram channel, agentRuntime: claude-cli routing on a canonical `anthropic/<model>` alias):

**Before (unpatched dist on 2026.6.5):**

```
/btw test1
→ ⚠️ /btw failed: Unknown model: anthropic/<model>
```

(matches the original report in #92168 — primitive `Unknown model: …` with no recovery hint)

**After step 1 (only the dist patched, no config change):**

```
/btw test1
→ ⚠️ /btw failed: Unknown model: anthropic/<model>.
   Found agents.defaults.models["anthropic/<model>"], but no matching
   models.providers["anthropic"].models[] entry. Add { "id": "<model>" }
   to models.providers["anthropic"].models[] to register this provider model.
```

The error string changes from the primitive `resolveModelWithRegistry` throw at the old `btw.ts:267` to the rich `buildUnknownModelError` produced by `resolveModelAsync`. This is exactly the resolver-path swap the patch makes — the same downstream error formatter the main agent loop already uses.

**After step 2 (dist patched + minimal `models.providers["anthropic"]` entry added per the new error's hint):**

```
/btw test1
→ <Yoyo's normal side-question answer streamed in, no ⚠️ banner;
   the configured CLI backend was invoked end-to-end and Anthropic
   returned tokens normally>
```

`/btw` now follows the same resolver path as a normal main-loop turn, picks up the same model, and reaches the same CLI backend.

After capturing the proof I restored the original dist and rolled back the temporary `models.providers["anthropic"]` entry, so the only persistent change on the host is what this PR contains.

## Tests

- `npm test -- --run src/agents/btw.test.ts`
- `npm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts`
- `npm run tsgo:core:test`
- `npx oxfmt --check src/agents/btw.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts`
- `npx oxlint src/agents/btw.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `npm run check:changed` could not run locally because the configured Blacksmith/crabbox wrapper failed its own binary sanity check before executing repository checks.
target.author: wangwllu
